content.claim: Hungary faces structural teacher shortage driven by low wages (among lowest in Europe) and high student-teacher ratios, with teachers performing non-teaching duties due to insufficient support staff
content.context: Fresh live research through the Implementation planning lens
limitations: Direct link between teacher shortage and capacity to implement structural reforms not empirically quantified
International wage comparisons may not account for purchasing power or cost-of-living differences
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
